cityDNA.coreFantasy: A dragonkin city where Shelter Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place, Military Command, Prison / Containment, Harbor Campus, and Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath turn coastal edge, seawall edge, harbor basin, coast and lava rock, basalt, obsidian, gold leaf, glazed tile into a distinct civic machine.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Vyrath’Kai should feel like a dragonkin bastion shaped by coast, heat, oaths, shelter, command, and guarded rites.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Vyrath’Kai organizes public life around guardianship of what must survive: its districts convert Shelter Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place, Military Command, Prison / Containment, Harbor Campus into a readable system of authority, labor, memory, and movement.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Vyrath’Kai should be recognized by Lava Rock, Basalt, Obsidian, Gold Leaf, Glazed Tile, Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, Coast, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Draconic Heat, Threshold Oaths, Salt Wards, and the named anchors Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath, Shelter Hall Beacon, and Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: The city belongs to dragonkin; its civic habits should grow from the sidecar-backed district spine rather than generic fantasy urbanism.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath, Shelter Hall Beacon, Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion, and the five section landmark families symbolize guardianship of what must survive while warning against protection curdling into containment and command.

canonLocks: Vyrath’Kai is a dragonkin city, not a generic settlement with cosmetic motifs.
The Wonder is Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath.
The Beacon is Shelter Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion, located at the District 13 authority center.
The five district sections are Shelter Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place, Military Command, Prison / Containment, Harbor Campus.
The board should be treated as twenty-five district slots grouped into five section identities.
Section 1 (Shelter Hall) preserves these exact anchor names: S1 Shelter Hall, Shelter Hall Beacon, S1 Shelter Pillar, S1 Shelter Kiosk.
Section 2 (Shrine / Sacred Place) preserves these exact anchor names: Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook.
Section 3 (Military Command) preserves these exact anchor names: Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Woven Banners Monument, Standard Tower Nook.
Section 4 (Prison / Containment) preserves these exact anchor names: Prison / Containment Civic Hall, Containment Keep, Mist Veils Monument, Judgment Yard Nook.
Section 5 (Harbor Campus) preserves these exact anchor names: S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet.
provisionalCanon: Individual district-slot names beyond exact sidecar anchors are organizational labels for the 25-seat board, not new landmark canon.
Minor street customs should be derived from the section spine unless future source files provide more exact anchors.
rejectedReads: Vyrath’Kai as a generic fantasy city without its named Wonder, Beacon, and Seat of Power.
Replacing exact sidecar anchor names with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, wall, or archive.
Treating district section labels as decorative instead of civic systems that drive scenes and assets.
cautionFlags: Preserve exact named anchors from sidecars.
Use the culture identity respectfully as civic design language rather than costume.
Keep all 25 district slots legible even when five slots share one section identity.
Do not add unsupported individual district landmark names beyond the source-backed anchors.

environment.environmentalLogic: Vyrath’Kai's environment should make Shelter Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place, Military Command, Prison / Containment, Harbor Campus feel physically necessary. Terrain, light, civic routes, and landmark placement must support movement through all five section identities rather than read as interchangeable scenery.

skyline: Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath is the wonder-scale skyline identifier.
Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion marks the central authority mass at District 13.
Shelter Hall Beacon must remain visible as the public signal landmark.
Section landmarks should form a readable ring of Shelter Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place, Military Command, Prison / Containment, Harbor Campus.

civilization.people: Vyrath’Kai's people are dragonkin; they should be written through shared civic habits, public spaces, work rhythms, and the visible demands of the five section identities.
socialClasses: officials and stewards tied to Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion
landmark keepers and civic hall administrators
street vendors, porters, runners, and maintenance crews
families living around the section-specific local anchors
civilization.governance: Authority centers on Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion and is made visible through section civic halls, named landmarks, permissions, public signals, and the citywide response to Shelter Hall Beacon.
civilization.religion: Religious or ceremonial life should be shown through source-backed shrines, temples, wonders, oath spaces, ancestor sites, or ritual geometry when those appear in the district spine.
customs: citizens orient themselves by the section identity of a street before they identify an address
public notices and signals are interpreted through the Beacon and civic halls
named landmarks act as meeting points, proof points, and local memory anchors
section-specific work routines shape food, clothing, posture, and street traffic
District 13 authority matters even when scenes occur in outer district slots
bodyLanguage: citizens check landmarks and signals before crossing into another section
officials use the posture and tools of their section role
workers treat section anchors as practical wayfinding points
crowds become more formal near the Seat of Power and more local near minor landmarks
speechPatterns: directions use exact landmark names rather than generic descriptions
official speech references permits, seals, routes, oaths, or signals as appropriate to the city
working speech is concrete, location-heavy, and tied to civic function
locals distinguish between the Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section landmarks without confusion

streets.appearance: Street scenes should make Vyrath’Kai immediately identifiable through Lava Rock, Basalt, Obsidian, Gold Leaf, Glazed Tile, Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, Coast, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Draconic Heat, Threshold Oaths, Salt Wards, and the exact section anchors rather than generic fantasy facades.
streets.movement: Movement flows through five section identities and twenty-five district slots. People navigate by Shelter Hall Beacon, Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion, section civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks.
streets.lighting: Lighting should use Lantern Lit, Civic Blue Glow, Mist Veils, White Limestone Civic Blocks, Forge Smoke Columns, district lanterns, with the Beacon and Seat carrying distinct civic emphasis.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should interact with coastal edge, seawall edge, harbor basin, coast and the city's source-backed materials, altering visibility, travel speed, public gathering, and signal readability.
streets.dayCycle: Daytime emphasizes section labor: Shelter Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place, Military Command, Prison / Containment, Harbor Campus. Civic halls, shops, and public works create the main public rhythm.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes should preserve the city's identity through landmark silhouettes, beacon light, watch routes, late shops, ritual spaces, and section-specific sounds.

prestigious: workshops or offices near Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion
specialist houses serving Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath
master artisans tied to section landmarks

housing.wealthy: High-status housing clusters near Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion, major terraces, and prestigious landmark approaches; it should display the city materials cleanly and control sightlines toward Wonder or Beacon.
housing.middle: Middle housing occupies upper rooms, side courts, work-family compounds, and mixed-use streets close to civic halls and section services.
housing.poor: Poorer housing should be practical and specific to local labor, with patched materials, shared courtyards, crowded lanes, and strong dependence on nearby section anchors.

wonder.name: Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of guardianship of what must survive made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A wonder-scale skyline anchor using Lava Rock, Basalt, Obsidian, Gold Leaf, Glazed Tile, Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, Coast, and the dragonkin visual language.
wonder.civicRole: Represents the whole city above the five sections: Shelter Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place, Military Command, Prison / Containment, Harbor Campus.
storyHooks: access to Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath changes public legitimacy
a citywide ritual reveals hidden pressure
the Wonder becomes a navigation or warning reference

beacon.name: Shelter Hall Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public signal and shared alarm for Vyrath’Kai.
beacon.visualRole: A section-scale signal landmark that must read immediately as the city Beacon.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates public response, civic warnings, and Conjunction-state attention.
storyHooks: someone controls the first interpretation of a signal
the Beacon redirects traffic between sections
a warning exposes which citizens trust civic authority

seatOfPower.name: Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion

seatOfPower.visualRole: The District 13 authority complex with formal approaches and city-specific materials.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Seat of government, petitions, records, rulings, and command visibility.
storyHooks: a ruling contradicts a section custom
a sealed record changes public interpretation

districtDioramaGuidance: Create twenty-five district dioramas grouped into five recognizable sections: Shelter Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place, Military Command, Prison / Containment, Harbor Campus.
Each district should use its districtNumber and sectionNumber, but avoid baked text labels in imagery.
D13 must read as the Seat of Power district centered on Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion.
Repeated section anchors may appear across five related slots, but each slot needs its own street function, crowd density, and prop emphasis.
Use exact source-backed anchor names for file naming, prompt references, and canon cross-checking.
S1 must preserve Shelter Hall with anchors: S1 Shelter Hall, Shelter Hall Beacon, S1 Shelter Pillar, S1 Shelter Kiosk.
S2 must preserve Shrine / Sacred Place with anchors: Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook.
S3 must preserve Military Command with anchors: Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Woven Banners Monument, Standard Tower Nook.
S4 must preserve Prison / Containment with anchors: Prison / Containment Civic Hall, Containment Keep, Mist Veils Monument, Judgment Yard Nook.
S5 must preserve Harbor Campus with anchors: S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Map backgrounds should keep Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath as the skyline symbol, Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion at the central civic authority, and Shelter Hall Beacon as the readable public signal.
Arrange the five section identities so Vyrath’Kai does not converge with other cities: Shelter Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place, Military Command, Prison / Containment, Harbor Campus.

Use terrain cues (Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, Coast), material cues (Lava Rock, Basalt, Obsidian, Gold Leaf, Glazed Tile), and magic cues (Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Draconic Heat, Threshold Oaths, Salt Wards) consistently across district clusters.
Show roads, terraces, gates, courts, or paths that make the twenty-five-district tactical layout plausible.
elementsToAvoid: extra top-level schema sections or embedded generation notes
generic fantasy city assets that ignore exact anchor names
unlabeled invented landmarks replacing sidecar names
flattening five sections into one visual district
treating culture identity as costume rather than civic design
overcrowded detail that hides Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power
